description: The Alkhalil family is a Palestinian Canadian organized crime family based in Surrey, British Columbia. Originating from Palestinian refugees who fled to Lebanon and later Saudi Arabia, they immigrated to Canada in 1990. The family has been involved in gang violence, including a long-standing feud with the Duhre brothers. Several family members have been murdered or convicted of crimes.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 12 December 1990, the Alkhalil family immigrated to Canada as refugees... The family settled in Surrey , British Columbia.
country_links.context: The Alkhalil family immigrated to Canada as refugees and settled in Surrey, British Columbia, where they engaged in gang activities including stabbings, drug trafficking, and murders.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2015, he was arrested in Colombia when the airport officials at the Bogotá airport discovered he had arrived with a forged Canadian passport.
country_links.context: Nabil Alkhalil was arrested in Colombia when airport officials discovered he arrived with a forged Canadian passport.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 23 February 2013, Alkhalil was arrested in Greece where he had been living under a forged French passport and working as a haute couture designer.
country_links.context: Alkhalil was arrested in Greece where he had been living under a forged French passport.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2013, Nabil Alkhalil went to Mexico on a forged Canadian passport. He worked at an used car dealership in Ciudad López Mateos while continuing to be active in organised crime. Nabil Alkhalil is believed to have served as the Alkhalil family's liaison with the Sinaloa Cartel .
country_links.context: Nabil Alkhalil went to Mexico on a forged passport, worked at a used car dealership, and served as the family's liaison with the Sinaloa Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 7 September 2025, it was reported that Alkhalil had been arrested in Qatar.
country_links.context: Alkhalil was arrested in Qatar, indicating the organization's presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 19 September 2025, Potvin was arrested in Spain on an Interpol red notice issued by Canada.
country_links.context: An associate of the Alkhalil family was arrested in Spain on an Interpol red notice.

country_links.evidence_quote: Alkhalil was involved in the spring of 2012 in a deal to bring in 200 kilograms of cocaine worth $5 million from Mexico into Canada via Chicago
country_links.context: The Wolfpack Alliance, led by Rabih Alkhalil, smuggled cocaine from Mexico into Canada via the United States, specifically through Chicago.
